new_classes.definition_en: A spirituous beverage manufactured from sugar-cane molasses or from the sweet juices of plants and rice, using a special yeast, and then distilled. Arrack is distinct from raki (obtained by redistilling raisin or dry fig spirits with aniseed, classified under CN 2208 90 56 or 2208 90 69).
A distillation process in which a special yeast is used to ferment sugar-cane molasses or sweet plant juices combined with rice, followed by distillation to produce arrack.
Sugar-cane molasses or sweet juices of plants combined with rice, used as the fermentation feedstock in the production of arrack.
A quality of a spirit indicating that its fermentation was carried out using a special (distinctive) yeast strain, as required for the production of arrack.

new_classes.label_de: Arrak in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von ≤ 2 L (KN 2208 90 11)
new_classes.definition_en: Arrack presented in containers of a nominal capacity not exceeding 2 litres, classified under CN subheading 2208 90 11.

new_properties.label_en: has molasses or plant juice feedstock

coverage_explanation:
The proposed axioms cover the main distinguishing criteria for CN 2208 90 11:
(1) Arrack as a class of SpiritousBeverage — covered.
(2) Production from sugar-cane molasses or sweet plant juices combined with rice — partially covered via SugarCaneMolassesFeedstock and hasMolassesFeedstock; the rice component is described in the definition but not modelled as a separate part restriction to avoid over-engineering.
(3) Use of a special yeast — covered via SpecialYeastFermentationQuality.
(4) Container size ≤ 2 L for the 22089011 subheading — covered via ArrackInSmallContainer with has_part SmallContainer.
(5) Distinction from raki (CN 2208 90 56/69) — noted in definitions but not expressed as a complement/disjointness axiom, since Raki is not yet modelled in the TBox; this is a gap.
Coverage is slightly reduced because: (a) the rice ingredient is captured only in prose, not as a separate OWL restriction; (b) the disjointness with Raki cannot be formally expressed without a Raki class in the TBox.
